name: Threshold Network
in_scope: Direct theft of any user funds, whether at-rest or in-motion, other than unclaimed yield (Critical)
Temporary freezing of funds for at least 1 hour (High)
Smart contract unable to operate due to lack of token funds (Medium)
Griefing (e.g. no profit motive for an attacker, but damage to the users or the protocol) (Medium)

critical_contracts.name: TBTC
critical_contracts.description: Main tBTC ERC20 token representing Bitcoin on Ethereum

critical_contracts.name: TBTCVault
critical_contracts.description: Custodian for minted tBTC, manages minting and unminting operations

critical_contracts.name: Bridge
critical_contracts.description: Core Bitcoin bridge logic, handles deposits and redemptions

critical_contracts.name: WalletRegistry
critical_contracts.description: Manages ECDSA wallet groups for threshold signing

critical_contracts.name: T
critical_contracts.description: Threshold Network governance and utility token

attack_surfaces: Token vending machine (KEEP/NU to T migration)

high_priority_vulnerabilities.description: Forge or manipulate Bitcoin SPV proofs to mint unauthorized tBTC
Mint tBTC without depositing Bitcoin via optimistic minting mechanism
Bypass threshold signature requirements to control Bitcoin funds
Forge cross-chain messages to Wormhole or Starknet bridges
Manipulate staking reward calculations to inflate rewards
Use flash loans to manipulate governance votes

out_of_scope: Any testing on mainnet or public testnet deployed code
Any testing with pricing oracles or third-party smart contracts
Phishing or social engineering attacks against project's employees and/or customers
Testing with third-party systems and applications
Denial of service attacks that are executed against project assets
Automated testing of services that generates significant amounts of traffic
Public disclosure of an unpatched vulnerability in an embargoed bounty
Any other actions prohibited by the Immunefi Rules
Incorrect data supplied by third party oracles (except oracle manipulation/flash loan attacks)
Impacts requiring basic economic and governance attacks (e.g. 51% attack)
Lack of liquidity impacts
Impacts from Sybil attacks
Impacts involving centralization risks
Impacts requiring attacks that the reporter has already exploited themselves, leading to damage
Impacts caused by attacks requiring access to leaked keys/credentials
Impacts caused by attacks requiring access to privileged addresses without additional modifications
Impacts relying on attacks involving the depegging of an external stablecoin
Mentions of secrets, access tokens, API keys, private keys in Github without proof of production use
Best practice recommendations
Feature requests
Impacts on test files and configuration files unless stated otherwise
rules: All testing must be done on local-forks of either public testnet or mainnet
Proof of Concept is required for all severities
No KYC required for payouts under $100,000
Rewards distributed according to Immunefi Vulnerability Severity Classification System V2.3
Critical smart contract vulnerabilities capped at 10% of funds at risk
Payouts are denominated in USD and made in USDC, USDT, ETH, or T tokens
All payouts are handled by the Threshold Network team
Duplicates and private known issues are not eligible for rewards

rewards.critical: Up to $1,000,000 (10% of funds at risk) - Direct theft, permanent freezing, protocol insolvency
rewards.high: Up to $50,000 - Theft/freezing of unclaimed yield, temporary fund freezing (1+ hours)
rewards.medium: Up to $10,000 - Contract inoperability, block stuffing, griefing, gas theft, unbounded gas
rewards.low: Up to $1,000 - Based on Immunefi Severity Classification System

notes: Threshold Network is a merge of Keep Network and NuCypher, providing threshold cryptography for decentralized applications. The primary products are tBTC (a decentralized Bitcoin bridge) and threshold access control. This bug bounty program focuses on smart contract security across 39 deployed contracts on Ethereum mainnet. All testing must be done on local forks to avoid impact on production systems.
